author: @katochh

text: VEGAS, which used to be "Sony VEGAS", but is now branded as "MAGIX VEGAS" and available e.g. through Steam as a digital license. Once you get the interface and keyboard shortcuts down -- and once you've tweaked the (Internal) Preferences to fit your particular PC/system -- then you'll find that it's technically-precise and powerful, highly versatile, software. Learn the nuances, and it's smooth sailing. I have not been paid to say this, and this sentiment might be best described as a certain manifestation of "Stockholm Syndrome".
author: @irover

text: This was one of the first Intensive Care Unit YTPMVs I listened to. It's still awesome now as it was then.
author: @AM-yu9wy

text:
Thanks, friendo. Technically (a/v complexity, emphasis on audio quality/fidelity-mimetics), I think this was among the top two YTPMVs I made whilst active. Certainly, it contains the greatest number of (*non-track-split*) samples/timeline-events -- by far -- of any of mine vids. I recall having both great fun and great difficulty with the 'percussive breakdown' midway through the song... Which, again, and as was emphasized above, was/is not able to be split apart or otherwise formally and conveniently decomposed into isolable audio tracks. As such, this is, IMHO (where the H stands for "horrible"), probably the peak of my technical output within the realm of "True YTPMVs" -- though, in a sociotechnomeritocratic tragedy, this means nothing whatsoever beyond the outermost edges of The Fartist's Ego, A.K.A. @me lmao nobody cares :^)

...but thanks for commenting what you did! I will finally reiterate my indefinite gratitude to Sheikmaster32 for having rendered this video (in HD!!) on my behalf way back when; I could not do do on my PC, which had 1gb VRAM and would not even show me any video preview towards the end of the project, compositionally speaking. SM32 could've just, say, rendered the vid and claimed credit or otherwise capriciously screwed with me... and yet, rather than somehow seizing the moment to his own advantage/whimsy, he rendered the .veg precisely as requested and returned to me the final video (as uploaded hereinabove) with nary a moment's dawdling nor a modicum of malice. So long as I <[mtr]> maintain this account, this fact will retain its emphasis, for sheikmaster32 will always have my gratitude. Good guy. One of the best of that old YTPMV Skype "crew" -- but that rambling subject would be best reserved for some other time.

Thanks again for your comment -- ah, hmm, uhhh, and I do hope I'm pronouncing this correctly -- "at-A-M-dash-y-u-9-w-y". Stay safe, remain in light, oppose all pavement, and godspeed. :^) (1)
